skill.name: autonomous-ci
skill.description: Guides an AI coding assistant to verify local tests, push code, monitor GitHub Actions, and report completion only with evidence.
skill.summary: Require local test and GitHub Actions evidence before completion claims.
skill.author: ANcpLua

security_audit.summary: The external command findings are mostly true positives because the skill instructs the agent to run tests, Git commands, and GitHub CLI commands. The network findings are partly true for GitHub CLI and push behavior, while hardcoded GitHub URLs are examples and the weak cryptography detections are false positives. No prompt injection attempt or malicious exfiltration intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Agent Executes Project and CI Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The skill instructs the assistant to run project test commands, Git commands, and GitHub CLI commands. This is expected for a CI verification skill, but it can execute repository scripts and remote CI operations. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: Multiple explicit command blocks are present and match the stated purpose, but no malicious command or user input injection pattern is shown.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Multiple explicit command blocks are present and match the stated purpose, but no malicious command or user input injection pattern is shown.

medium_findings.title: Broad Git Staging, Commit, and Push Workflow
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The skill tells the assistant to run git add ., git commit, and git push after local tests pass. This can publish unintended files or secrets if the repository state is not reviewed first. Confidence: 0.84. Confidence reasoning: The commands are directly shown and can change local and remote repository state, but they are not hidden or malicious.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly shown and can change local and remote repository state, but they are not hidden or malicious.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The referenced lines do not contain weak cryptographic algorithm use. They contain skill metadata, prose about a generic test runner, and a sample git commit command. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: Direct review of the exact lines found no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or comparable weak algorithm usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct review of the exact lines found no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or comparable weak algorithm usage.

low_findings.title: Example GitHub URLs Are Not Exfiltration Endpoints
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The hardcoded URLs appear only inside an example success report and do not instruct data transfer to a third-party endpoint. Confidence: 0.9. Confidence reasoning: The URLs are illustrative GitHub Actions run links in report text, with no HTTP client or credential handling around them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are illustrative GitHub Actions run links in report text, with no HTTP client or credential handling around them.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution in CI Monitoring Example
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The shell example uses command substitution to capture commit, run, and conclusion values. It is a normal shell workflow but should run only in a trusted repository and authenticated GitHub context. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The substitutions are explicit and bounded to git and gh commands, so the risk is operational rather than clearly malicious.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The substitutions are explicit and bounded to git and gh commands, so the risk is operational rather than clearly malicious.

content.value_statement: AI assistants can claim work is complete before local tests and remote CI actually pass. This skill creates a strict verification workflow with local test runs, GitHub Actions monitoring, and evidence-based reporting.
actual_capabilities: Defines a completion gate that requires local tests before success claims.
Lists common local test commands for .NET, Node.js, Python, and Go projects.
Shows how to monitor GitHub Actions runs with the GitHub CLI.
Requires failed CI logs to be reviewed before retrying verification.
Provides an evidence format for reporting local tests, CI status, workflow links, commit, and timestamp.
Highlights unsafe assistant rationalizations that should stop the workflow.
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI to be installed and authenticated.
Assumes the repository uses GitHub Actions for CI.
Does not provide executable scripts in this skill package.
Can stage and push unintended files if repository changes are not reviewed first.

use_cases.title: Pre-Completion Verification
use_cases.description: Require an assistant to run local tests and check CI before it says a code task is complete.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Readiness
use_cases.description: Confirm that a pushed commit has passing GitHub Actions before opening or updating a pull request.

use_cases.title: CI Failure Follow-Up
use_cases.description: Guide the assistant to inspect failed workflow logs, fix the issue, and repeat verification.

prompt_templates.title: Run Basic Verification
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner verification after a code change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the autonomous-ci workflow for this repository. Run the appropriate local tests, then tell me whether the work is ready for CI verification.

prompt_templates.title: Verify Before Completion
prompt_templates.prompt: Before you claim this task is complete, apply the autonomous-ci protocol. Include local test results, commit status, CI status, and evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Monitor GitHub Actions
prompt_templates.scenario: Remote CI check after pushing a commit
prompt_templates.prompt: After pushing the current commit, use GitHub CLI to find the workflow runs for that commit. Wait for completion and report every workflow conclusion.

prompt_templates.title: Recover From Failed CI
prompt_templates.prompt: CI failed for the latest commit. Use the autonomous-ci process to inspect failed logs, identify the root cause, fix it, rerun local tests, and verify CI again.

output_examples.input: Verify this fix before saying it is done.
output_examples.output: Local tests passed. The commit was pushed. GitHub Actions completed successfully for tests, lint, and build. The work is verified complete with workflow links and commit evidence.

output_examples.input: CI failed after the push. What should happen next?
output_examples.output: The assistant reviews failed workflow logs, identifies the failing step, applies a fix, reruns local tests, pushes a new commit, and waits for CI again.

output_examples.input: Can you skip CI because the change is small?
output_examples.output: No. The workflow requires local tests and passing remote CI before any completion claim.

best_practices: Review git status before staging or committing files.
Run the repository's established test and build commands first.
Include CI run links and final workflow conclusions in completion reports.
anti_patterns: Claiming completion after only local tests pass.
Running git add . without checking changed files.
Assuming CI will pass without watching the workflow result.

faq.question: Does this skill run CI by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides an instruction workflow for an assistant to run commands and verify GitHub Actions.

faq.question: Which CI provider does it support?
faq.answer: The documented workflow is built around GitHub Actions and the GitHub CLI.

faq.question: Does it support local tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists common commands for .NET, Node.js, Python, and Go projects.

faq.question: Is it safe to use in any repository?
faq.answer: Use caution. The workflow includes git add, git commit, git push, and repository test commands.

faq.question: What evidence should the assistant provide?
faq.answer: It should report local test results, CI conclusions, workflow links, commit ID, and timestamp.

faq.question: Can the assistant skip failed CI logs?
faq.answer: No. The workflow requires failed logs to be reviewed before fixing and retrying.
